metadata.scene_name: Collision between Blocks on a Frictional Surface
metadata.task: Make the two blocks collide on the frictional surface and compare rebound motion with the incoming motion. Return 1 if friction reduces rebound speed, 2 if rebound speed is unchanged, and 3 if rebound speed increases.
metadata.description: The blocks and surface all have appreciable friction. Drive the blocks into contact, then measure velocities after the collision before selecting the numeric rebound code.

expected_behavior: The LLM observes that friction slows the blocks after collision and reduces rebound speed.
reasoning: Friction dissipates kinetic energy during and after contact, so the numeric rebound code is 1.
